original text:
The Nokia 5300 is a music enabled mobile phone that promises captivating music and more to end-users. On first glance, one would like the way the handset looks. This sophisticated gadget comes in a compact exterior, a sleek slide open design and makes a positive impact on an onlooker.
The music options of the Nokia 5300 are quite varied and equally interesting. With the Nokia 5300, a world of fun and music is waiting to be explored. There in an integrated multimedia player, which can play music files in formats as diverse as MP3, Midi, AAC to AAC+, enhanced AAC+, and WMA. There are large and tactile keys to control the multimedia player � so it becomes quite easy for a person to enjoy his favourite music tracks, whenever and wherever he wants to. The sound output is quite good; and with dedicated volume keys � it is possible to personalize the listening experience to a large extent. In addition, there are headphones and stereo speakers that one could use. Moreover, a user could enjoy entertaining programs of the integrated FM radio.
A 1.3-megapixel camera with 8x digital zoom is another key feature of this versatile handset. The camera can be used to shoot photos in sequence mode; the best frames among those taken can then be selected and shared with family and friends. Bluetooth compatibility of the Nokia 5300 ensures easy transfer of the images and video clips to personal computers and printers.
As an owner of this amazing mobile phone, you are free to enjoy the sounds of the latest ringtones. You could, for instance, download your choice of mp3 ringtones, video ringtones, midi ringtones or true tones and give a definite identity to your handset. With up to 5MB of internal user memory, which you can enhance depending upon your specific needs, there is no dearth of storage space for all the music files, images, video clips, ringtones and other content.
Tri-band connectivity ensures that you remain connected in diverse areas of the globe. And with capabilities for web browsing and smart content downloads, the Nokia 5300 is as good as it gets.
Buy Nokia 5300 mobile phone from uk leading phone shop with Orange, T, O2 and Vodafone mobile phone networks.

At the conclusion of the Mailboxes Unlimited Winter Age Groups Championships at the BASA Pool, www.islandstats.com can bring you the Individual Points Age Group Winners.
Marleigh Howes was crowned the 10 & Under Girls Individual Champions after she recorded 1,385 points, Arabella Newport Derbyshire was second with 1,136 points and Ava Pedro finished third with 1,064 points.
The 10 & Under Boys Individual Champion was Luke Horan who had 631 points, Ramsay Soutter was second with 580 points and Jacob Wright finished third with 559 points.
Giada Dudley-Pun won the 11-12 Girls Individual title with 2,333 points, Imojen Judd finished second with 2,065 points and Bella Howes finished third with 1,926 points.
The 11-12 Boys Individual Champion was Brandon Adkins who had 1,700 points, Harlan Watson-Brown was second with 1,357 points and Flynn Watson-Brown finished third with 1,335 points.
Taylor White was crowned the 13-14 Girls Individual Champion after she recorded 2,705 points, Josephine Duerden was second with 2,336 points and Emma Kittleson finished third with 2,210 points.
The 13-14 Boys Individual crown was won by Finn Moseley who had 2,168 points, Tommy Marshall was second with 1,278 points and Leo Foster had 1,266 points finishing third.
Logan Watson-Brown was crowned the 15 & Over Girls - Individual Champion after she accumulated 3,181 points, Skyler Powell was second with 2,885 points and Alyson Scherer was third with 2,417 points.
The 15 & Over Boys Individual title went to Brian Desmond who had 2,768 points, Jack Harvey finished second with 2,506 and Zebedee Wakely finished third with 2,203 points.

I will go manual soon as I shave 200th of a second off my time to get my ghost up, iv never played with a wheel and manual so that my next task, my current time is 1.25.2?? My kids are on the ps at the moment so I can't remember the last 2 numbers, but I need to shave 200th of a second to get in the top 30, I got a 1.26.5?? With default tuning and I couldn't get any faster, iv also can't get any faster with my current setup giving me a 1.25.2??
Probably not the best way to fix understeer but I always end up playing with the diff settings. This is a basic overview of the diff changes that can be made. Here (http://forum.projectcarsgame.com/showthread.php?25676-Easy-Differential-Tuning-(reduce-spinouts)).
You could try softening the front AR/Sway bar, but just a click or two. Too soft and you'll have problems with body roll. If it's happening on corner entry, you can also try softening the front springs to get more weight on the front tires under deceleration.
I jumped 10 places last night shaving 800th of a second off my time putting me in 15th place out of the rwd lmp1's and 26th in the class, which I'm over the moon about and iv go my ghost up, I'm roughly 1.8 1.9 seconds behind the fastest lmp1 which is a life time in racing as you already know, im going to switch to going manual now and hopefully when I get the hang of it I can shave some more seconds off.
Although the right amount of accel diff helps rotate the car better out of certain corners to mitigate that exit-understeer.
But first: what Haiden said. I'd also recommend running the setup through Jussi's calculator for a more neutral/less front biased setup with appropriate damping.
As for entry well, what Haiden said. Again. If your braking super late and hot on entry the front tyres will give in. A bit of brake bias towards the rear a few clicks might help there, though if you're too aggressive it'll snap sideways on entry.

Kansas City, MO. (November 3, 2016)  -  Blue Cross and Blue Shield of Kansas City (Blue KC) has been certified as a Gold Level Healthy KC company by the Greater Kansas City Chamber of Commerce Healthy KC Workplace Wellness Certification program.
The Healthy KC workplace wellness certification is based on four 'pillars' of health: nutrition, physical activity, tobacco cessation, and work/life integration. There are four levels of certification, based on a company's level of achievement across the four pillars - Gold, Silver, Bronze, and Honorable Mention.
"This is the second year Blue KC has been honored with the Gold certification and we're proud to provide a healthy environment for our employees and to be a part of the larger Healthy KC initiative that is creating a healthier Kansas City for all," said Gregg Laiben, M.D., Blue KC, Vice President, Health Innovations.
"We'd also like to congratulate the 212 companies that were given the Healthy KC Workplace Wellness certification and encourage those who haven't applied to start thinking about ways to make their workplaces healthier and apply in 2017," Laiben added.
The certification process will be repeated each year, with the next round slated to open in early 2017. For more information, visit www.wearehealthykc.com or e-mail healthykc@kcchamber.com.

No summer vacation, road trip or overseas getaway is complete without at least one photo of an awkward or hilarious sign to share with your friends and coworkers when you get home. The S.F. Chronicle Travel section has collected quite a few of these silly sign photos over the years courtesy of Doug Lansky's Signspotting.com. Check them all out below.
You can upload your own funny signs at www.signspotting.com.
Stop by so you can tweet about twits who are treated royally. Montreal, Quebec, Canada.
From the looks of things, pork is facing an identity crisis at this dining establishment. Brooklyn, New York.
Do not take your baby to Hickory Island in Florida. Bonita Springs, Florida.
This takes combo deals to a whole new level. Mountain View, California.
Some cheese lovers might go bananas over this menu item, but everyone else is peeling off as fast as they can. Clifton Park, New York.
Sometimes the unknown tastes a bit gamey but the price is right. Location unknown.
In Las Vegas, the Babies Against Briefcases movement is alive and well. Las Vegas, Nevada.
Beware: Throwing away your apple core may result in an attack of vomiting. Canada.
Customers have their choice of regular or extra cold ice. Salem, Oregon.
We can only assume this is one of those weird English translation issues. You know, trousers versus pants, tinned versus canned, protected versus dead. London, United Kingdom.
Seems like the lifesaving aspect needs a bit of work. Pt. Reyes National Seashore, California.
Clean hands, however, are optional. South Louisiana.
Your tired caboose may be aching for a rest on a bed of sharp metal, but please refrain from resting atop this fence. Farmington, Utah.
You never know when golf is going to strike. Vienna, Austria.
Not-so-scenic overlook. Hoover Dam, Nevada.
No standing at the cab stand, either: Apparently, the trick is getting a running start and leaping into a moving taxi. Hong Kong.
Emergency Margarita Technician: As we all know, when you're vacation, an empty glass is a real crisis. Dominican Republic.
Ah, the perks of being The Messiah. Grand Rapids, Michigan.
But what? Is it pronounced just like it looks? Don't leave us hanging! Andhra Perish, India.
Slow customers are rewarded with privileged parking at this store. Fast customers can park across the street. Los Angeles, California.
If an American accent isn't exotic enough, this store can sell you an Israeli one. Washington, D.C.
At this fine establishment, you have your choice of being served - or served by - swine. Monroe, Indiana.
If you're looking for the glazed old-fashioned from hell, you'll find it at this late night haunt. Anaheim, California.
It's a bird. It's a plane. No, wait - it's... Toiletman? Freeway, between Hangzhou and Shanghai, China.
Advanced driving skills: the inverted U-turn. Cottage Grove, Minnesota.
All applicants must be at least somewhat dead. Langley, Canada.
Coming soon! The first zoo employee born in captivity. Natural Bridge, Virginia.
These days, there's only a budget for a courtesy phone jack at the airport in Ketchikan, Alaska.
Please keep an eye on your children -- or they may learn to fly. Location unknown.
To not find Texas Street, make a left and continue for 7 miles. Albany, Oregon.
The designers of the earth friendly toilets at this restroom make users choose between green or clean. Wausau, Wisconsin.
If you think unsightly plantar warts are bad, you should see the frackle-warts they treat at this clinic. Vientiane, Laos.
It's not easy to prevent recreation at a youth center but it's a point of pride at this institute. La Conner, Washington.
If you have to apologize before your customers even make it inside, you may want rethink your business plan. Cabo San Lucas, Mexico.
Beware the slow-moving, shoulder-less children. Cairn, Australia.
Happy-go-lucky pedestrians only! Cave Creek, Arizona.
If you must slip at least do it with some grace and forethought. Chengadu, China.
Just try not to think about how one gets the required milk to make rat cheese. Cherokee, North Carolina.
Must have been opposite day. Cliffs of Moher, Ireland.
We don't know where this road leads either. Dead Sea, Israel.
A bike trail with the promise of adventure! Eagle River, Wisconsin.
This is rarely a good idea, no matter where you are. Ettal, Germany.
What kind of bogus 'entry' is this, anyway? Granada, Spain.
Solid advice: Don't park your car without being inside of it first. Hua Hin, Thailand.
Truth in advertising. London, England.
C'mon, everyone knows 'thank you' is actually 'ruff, ruff' in dog. Marmora, Ontario. Canada.
Gas passers will be prosecuted. Victoria City, British Columbia, Canada.
Danger: falling cars! Akumal, Mexico.
Unless you want to fork over $100, you better put some serious thought into where you 'relieve your self' around these parts. Antigua, Virgin Islands.
You can't say they didn't warn you. Atlas, Illinois.
Don't learn this the hard way. Austin, Texas.

Newswise  -  October 27, 2017 - Plastic surgeons using social media to attract patients should know their audience's preferred social media platforms and the types of posts of greatest interest, according to a survey study in the November issue of Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery®, the official medical journal of the American Society of Plastic Surgeons (ASPS).
"Plastic surgeons can utilize social media best by considering their target audience's perspective," said Heather Furnas, MD, of Plastic Surgery Associates of Santa Rosa, Calif. Dr. Furnas is a member of the American Society of Plastic Surgeons. The survey also suggests that patients rely on the plastic surgery practice's website over social media when seeking important information.
Dr. Furnas and coauthors surveyed 100 patients making visits to their aesthetic plastic surgery practice regarding social media habits and preferences. The patients, average age 44 years, were nearly all women. Most were interested in facial plastic surgery; some were interested in breast, body, or other cosmetic procedures.
Most patients used YouTube and Pinterest, but engagement was low. While only about one-fourth of patients were on Snapchat, most of them used it daily. Twitter was the least popular social media platform.
The plastic surgery practice's website beat out all social media platforms as the go-to source of online information. More than half of patients said they were influenced by the website when selecting a cosmetic surgery practice, compared to just eight percent for Facebook. More than 60 percent of patients checked the practice website on the day of their visit.
Out of 11 social media post categories, most patients chose before-and-after photos of cosmetic surgery procedures. More than one-fourth wanted to see information about the procedures; few selected didactic types of information.
Plastic surgeons can better reach and engage with their target population if they use the social media networks popular with their patients' demographic. For example, women prefer Facebook, Instagram and Pinterest, but Facebook in particular is most popular among the age group of women most likely to be interested in plastic surgery. While Twitter is widely used by plastic surgeons to share and discuss research, the new survey suggests it's the least popular platform among cosmetic surgery patients.
The fleeting content on social media may be less important than the information provided by the plastic surgeon's practice website.
"These results suggest that the website should be considered the centerpiece of a practice's online content," Dr. Furnas and coauthors write. "Social media should be viewed as an adjunct to attract and engage users, enticing them to explore the practice website."
Wolters Kluwer reported 2016 annual revenues of €4.3 billion. The company, headquartered in Alphen aan den Rijn, the Netherlands, serves customers in more than 180 countries, maintains operations in more than 40 countries and employs 19,000 people worldwide.
Wolters Kluwer Health is a leading global provider of information and point of care solutions for the health care industry.
